channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Crimson Desert Character Creator Mod Adds 98 Face Presets and 159 Hair Styles to Create Your Custom Characters
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert's thin narrative and shallow characters made more than a few fans wonder why Pearl Abyss did not allow players to create their own characters to explore Pywell. While the developer has yet to comment on the matter or provide the option, PC players can now create their own characters thanks to a new mod now available for download from Nexus Mods.

"This mod let's you build your own character by using the barber," says the mod's official description. "This mod allows you to create your own character with extensive customization options. It includes 98 face presets and 159 hairstyles, giving you a wide variety of unique looks. You can customize your character at the barber."

As the Character Creator mod, which is currently limited to a female option with a male option coming later today, modifies the barber mechanics, it is not compatible with any other mod that does the same, such as Barber Unlocked. In addition, the Kliff Female Voice mod is required for the character creator to work, as is the JSON Mod Manager.

This first release of the Crimson Desert Character Creator mod is only the beginning. Alongside the aforementioned male option, animation replacement is also in the works to make female characters feel like proper replacements for Kliff.

Will The Character Creator Ever Come to Consoles?

While Crimson Desert PC players now have the option to create their own custom characters, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S players are out of luck as they cannot use mods such as those that enhance the game's visuals with grass shadows and improved ray tracing.

However, developer Pearl Abyss not only has developed a solid character creation system for Black Desert, but it has also been highly responsive to player feedback. As such, there's still a good chance that, if they receive enough requests, the developer could add an official character creation system down the line.

Even without this feature, however, Crimson Desert is an open-world game worth experiencing, thanks to the massive amount of content and one of the best combat systems seen in similar games. If you are still traveling through Pywel, check out our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough & Guide for all the help you need to best some of the game's most intense challenges.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/crimson-desert-character-creator/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Update 1.23.0 Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has just released Patch 1.23.0 for ARC Raiders, and it’s a relatively small update that focuses on bug fixes across numerous aspects of the game. That being said, a new cosmetic set has arrived in the store to sweeten the deal, with the Vanguard Set now available for purchase.

This update comes after the highly anticipated Flashpoint update, which added a variety of new content to the game, including a new Major Map Condition and ARC threat: the devastating Vaporizer.

ARC Raiders Updates Numerous Bugs & Close Scrutiny

Live service titles like ARC Raiders are bound to be hit with new waves of bugs and glitches fairly regularly, thanks to the constant arrival of updates and content. Some can be silly, whilst others can be match-ruining, and update 1.23.0 impacts all of the above.

Some of the standout fixes include a return to form for the Rocketeer, as it will now sound its normal combat alert sound instead of the Vaporizers, as well as some AI adjustments for ARC during the Close Scrutiny Major Map Condition.

There’s plenty more in isolation, so for those of you interested in the minutia, here’s the full ARC Raiders Update 1.23.0 Patch Notes.

Full ARC Raiders Update 1.23.0 Patch Notes

ARC:

  • Fixed an issue that caused Rocketeers to use an incorrect combat alert sound.

Gameplay:

  • Fixed an issue where two Trigger ’Nades would sometimes deal three times the damage when detonated next to each other.
  • Fixed an issue that would cause items to not be used as soon as possible when holding fire and swapping to them.
  • Fixed an issue where weapons and items would fire automatically.
  • Fixed a shoulder swap issue where the camera would stay only on one side.
  • Fixed an exploit where an anvil splitter could be used without the penalty for dispersion.
  • Barricades can no longer be placed on Ziplines.

Maps:

  • Fixed an issue where players could get set on fire outside of the locked room in Medical Research on Stella Montis.
  • Fixed a spot on Stella Montis where players could be shot through the floor.
  • Fixed an issue where some staircases were missing in Buried City.
  • Fixed some instances where the code printer could not be interacted with during Locked Gate.

Close Scrutiny:

  • Changed the movement speed for Rocketeers and Vaporizers so they don’t behave strangely while idling.
  • Fixed an issue where reconnected players would not see Assessor platforms correctly.
  • Fixed an issue where Vaporizers would get stuck when getting close to an Assessor.
  • Fixed an issue that would prevent an Assessor from flying back into orbit. Fixed an issue where breach platforms could break on an Assessor when spamming the Breach & Search prompts preventing it from returning to orbit.
  • All ARC summoned by the Assessor will now correctly leave the area and despawn if they remain idle for a long time.
  • Pinging an Assessor in the map screen now correctly reflects the actual location.
  • Fixed an issue where players could get stuck when jumping from one platform to another on an Assessor.
  • Fixed a spot in Buried city where an Assessor and a Probe could land in the same spot.

Other:

  • Fixed an issue where the weapon upgrade UI would show incorrect upgrade tiers.
  • Fixed an issue that caused fully transparent helmet visors.
  • Fixed some instances where certain visual effects could cause crashes. We are continuing to investigate all cases that cause crashes.

What do you make of the ARC Raiders Update 1.23.0 Patch Notes? Did they miss any bugs you’re encountering? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the full reveal of the Forza Horizon 6 map, as well as the one-man Tarkov competitor, which has launched to thousands of positive reviews.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/arc-raiders-update-1-23-0-full-patch-notes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Full Japan Map Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Playground Games has just revealed the Full Summer Japan map for Forza Horizon 6.

The next installment of the highly popular racing series, Forza Horizon 6, is all set to arrive on May 16, 2026. Ahead of the launch, the developers have already revealed the official minimum to extreme PC system requirements for the game. Now, players can have a look at all the areas that they will be able to explore in the Japan map.

Forza Horizon 6 Japan Summer Map Revealed

A new post on the official Forza Horizon X account (formerly Twitter) reveals the entire map that players can expect in the game. It covers a huge area, from the Alps’ snow-covered hills to Tokyo’s bustling city roads to peaceful country lanes. One detail that players will notice is that the map says ‘Summer Season’, which could mean that the areas and POIs may change with seasonal map updates. Players will have to wait and see how that system works.

As we all know, the latest installment to the Forza Horizon series takes us to the breathtaking landscapes of Japan. The developers have also confirmed that this is their “most dense and vertical map yet.” The game will also immerse you in Japanese car culture, with over 550 real-world cars, including “classics, featuring cutting-edge engine audio and updated steering animations with up to 540 degrees of wheel rotation.”

In other news, Game Pass subscribers still have to pay $60 for Forza Horizon 6’s 4-day early access. Additionally, gameplay footage for Forza Horizon 6 has surfaced online, but it reveals a critical flaw in the game’s traffic density. What are your thoughts on the Forza Horizon 6 full Japan map reveal? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about over 300,000 players entering Gray Zone Warfare to explore the recent Spearhead update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/forza-horizon-6-full-japan-map-revealed/
